title: Fix kubevuln crash-looping due to ephemeral storage eviction
description:
**Problem:** kubevuln pods were repeatedly evicted because their ephemeral local storage usage exceeded the 10Gi limit. The vulnerability database downloads and image scan caches consumed more than the allocated ephemeral storage, causing a cycle of eviction and restart on worker-2.

**Solution:** Increased ephemeral storage limits via Helm upgrade from 10Gi to 50Gi (limit) and 5Gi to 10Gi (request), then cleaned up 24 dead pods left behind from the crash-loop cycle.

steps.title: Step 1
steps.description: Identified root cause: 'Pod ephemeral local storage usage exceeds the total limit of containers 10Gi' in pod events

steps.title: Step 2
steps.description: Ran Helm upgrade to increase ephemeral storage: helm upgrade kubescape-operator kubescape/kubescape-operator --namespace kubescape --reuse-values --set kubevuln.resources.requests.ephemeral-storage=10Gi --set kubevuln.resources.limits.ephemeral-storage=50Gi

steps.title: Step 3
steps.description: Cleaned up dead pods: kubectl -n kubescape delete pods --field-selector=status.phase==Failed

steps.title: Step 4
steps.description: Verified new pod running with updated limits: kubectl -n kubescape get pods -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubevuln

troubleshooting.title: CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
**Problem:** kubevuln pods were repeatedly evicted because their ephemeral local storage usage exceeded the 10Gi limit. The vulnerability database downloads and image scan caches consumed more than the allocated ephemeral storage, causing a cycle of eviction and restart on worker-2.

**Solution:** Increased ephemeral storage limits via Helm upgrade from 10Gi to 50Gi (limit) and 5Gi to 10Gi (request), then cleaned up 24 dead pods left behind from the crash-loop cycle.

resolution.summary:
**Problem:** kubevuln pods were repeatedly evicted because their ephemeral local storage usage exceeded the 10Gi limit. The vulnerability database downloads and image scan caches consumed more than the allocated ephemeral storage, causing a cycle of eviction and restart on worker-2.

**Solution:** Increased ephemeral storage limits via Helm upgrade from 10Gi to 50Gi (limit) and 5Gi to 10Gi (request), then cleaned up 24 dead pods left behind from the crash-loop cycle.
steps: Identified root cause: 'Pod ephemeral local storage usage exceeds the total limit of containers 10Gi' in pod events

Ran Helm upgrade to increase ephemeral storage: helm upgrade kubescape-operator kubescape/kubescape-operator --namespace kubescape --reuse-values --set kubevuln.resources.requests.ephemeral-storage=10Gi --set kubevuln.resources.limits.ephemeral-storage=50Gi
Cleaned up dead pods: kubectl -n kubescape delete pods --field-selector=status.phase==Failed
Verified new pod running with updated limits: kubectl -n kubescape get pods -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubevuln
resolution.yaml:
# Helm values override for kubevuln ephemeral storage
kubevuln:
  resources:
    requests:
      ephemeral-storage: 10Gi
    limits:
      ephemeral-storage: 50Gi
